title: Chelonistele pinniloba
intro: Chelonistele pinniloba, also known as the "leafy orchid," is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Chelonistele pinniloba has been used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Chelonistele pinniloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chelonistele pinniloba is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, although its use is not extensively documented in modern Western medicine.
taxonomy: Chelonistele pinniloba is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Chelonistele. The classification of Chelonistele pinniloba places it within the subfamily Aretoideae. More specifically, the species falls under the tribe Coelogyneae. Its classification is further detailed by its botanical name, which is often cited as Chelonistele pinniloba.
